title: December figures revealed for cargo boom at Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: The cargo boom at Prestwick Airport is showing no signs of slowing down following the release of figures from the end of 2025.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: December figures revealed for cargo boom at Prestwick Airport The airport has seen huge growth in its cargo operations thanks to a number of vital contracts (Image: Prestwick Airport)

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald. The South Ayrshire airport announced in December it had returned a £3.5 million operating profit for the year to March 31, 2025, up £300,000 from the previous year.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Cargo boom at Prestwick Airport showing no signs of slowing down
